name: Will using AEO software force me to redesign my website, or can it work with what I already have?

mainEntity.name: Will using AEO software force me to redesign my website, or can it work with what I already have?

acceptedAnswer.text:
AEO software typically integrates with your CMS, analytics, and tag manager, so it can suggest specific changes without requiring a full redesign. However, if your site is poorly structured or uses messy markup, you may need layout and template updates to fully benefit.

Answer engines prefer clear heading hierarchies, modular content blocks, and clean schema, all of which are easier with solid templates. The tool can point out problems; fixing them may involve dev or design work.

Key factors:
- Current CMS flexibility (WordPress, Webflow, custom, etc.)
- Ability to add JSON‑LD and adjust headings/sections
- Existing navigation and internal linking structure
- Mobile performance and accessibility basics

Start with incremental changes: improve headings, add FAQ blocks, and implement schema on a few templates, then expand to the whole site. If your CMS makes structured content difficult, factor that into your AEO software choice.

Use AEO findings as a roadmap for future UX and design improvements, not just SEO tweaks.
acceptedAnswer.description: Most AEO software works with your existing site, suggesting content, schema, and structural fixes rather than forcing a full redesign. If your templates are very rigid or messy, you may need some layout and markup updates to fully benefit from answer-focused optimization.
